objective: Enforce generator changed-files reporting against a captured baseline and current git-visible workspace state so lower agents cannot self-report arbitrary file lists.
blocking_rule: If the harness cannot capture a baseline for touched paths before coding, stop and write BLOCKED instead of guessing changed files after the fact.
in_scope: Capture a baseline snapshot for touched paths before generator work.
Make gate-close compare generator-reported changed files against actual changed files derived from baseline and git-visible workspace state.
Document the stricter baseline capture flow in the harness README.
out_of_scope: Product backend or frontend feature changes.
Replacing the harness with an always-on autonomous execution runtime.
Changing harness files outside the declared touched paths for this sprint.

acceptance_criteria.statement: gate-generator captures a baseline snapshot for the declared touched paths before coding begins.
gate-close fails if the generator report changed-file list does not match actual changed files from the baseline and git-visible dirty state.
The README explains that gate-generator must run before coding to capture the baseline.
The sprint-004 harness run passes under the stricter enforcement rules.

evaluator_checks: Baseline capture happens before coding for the declared touched paths.
Changed-file enforcement is based on measured state rather than self-report alone.
The stricter enforcement passes on a concrete sprint run.
